title: FDA Approves Casgevy — First Gene Therapy for Children With Sickle Cell Disease - CRBC News
summary:
FDA Approves
...
gevy —
...
The FDA has approved Casgevy as the first gene therapy for children aged 2 and older with sickle cell disease. Casgevy uses a patient’s own hematopoietic stem cells in a one‑time intravenous infusion to modify blood cell production. In a small pediatric study, eight of nine evaluable, transfusion‑dependent patients remained free of transfusions for 12 consecutive months. title: Setmelanotide shows promise for hypothalamic obesity after brain tumors
summary:
A new therapy helps achieve a healthier BMI in people whose weight has become dysregulated following treatment for a brain tumor, a clinical trial has found. The results of the trial have been published today in the prestigious New England Journal of Medicine. The new drug, setmelanotide, offers hope for improved health and quality of life for children and adults with hormonal and metabolic dysfunction caused by a brain tumor.

title: Saol Therapeutics Resubmits NDA for SL1009 in PDCD - cGxP.wire
summary: SL1009 (sodium dichloroacetate) is being developed as a precision therapy for Pyruvate Dehydrogenase Complex Deficiency (PDCD), a rare inherited mitochondrial disorder that causes severe energy metabolism dysfunction, chronic lactic acidosis, developmental impairment, neurological complications, and frequently early childhood death. summary:
Casgevy(exagamglogene autotemcel) is now approved in the U.S. to treat children as young as 2 with sickle cell disease(SCD) and recurrent vaso-occlusive crises (VOCs).
...
The U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) initially approved the Vertex Pharmaceuticals’ gene-editing therapy in 2023 for SCD patients ages 12 and older who experienced recurrent painful episodes known as VOCs. With this decision, Casgevy becomes the first gene-editing therapy approved for children as young as 2 with the dis

title: FDA Expands Indication For Vertex’s CASGEVY To Children Aged Two Years And Older - Healthcare - United States
summary: Vertex Pharmaceuticals has secured FDA approval to expand CASGEVY's indication to children as young as two years old with sickle cell disease or transfusion-dependent beta thalassemia, making it the first CRISPR-based gene-editing therapy available for this younger patient population. The approval, reviewed under the FDA's Commissioner's National Priority Voucher pilot program, follows recent European data presentations demonstrating efficacy and safety in pediatric patients. title: Setmelanotide Phase 3 Trial for Hypothalamic Obesity Published in NEJM
summary: A placebo-adjusted BMI reduction of 19.8 percentage points over 52 weeks is not a modest metabolic signal — it is the kind of number that reframes what treatment looks like for a disease defined by relentless, injury-driven weight gain. The Phase 3 TRANSCEND trial of setmelanotide in acquired hypothalamic obesity has now cleared peer review and landed in the New England Journal of Medicine, giving the dataset the scrutiny that regulatory filings alone cannot provide. summary: WASHINGTON, July 7, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Vanda Pharmaceuticals Inc. (Vanda) (Nasdaq: VNDA) today announced that the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has granted Rare Pediatric Disease Designation to VCA-894A, Vanda's investigational antisense oligonucleotide therapy for the treatment of Charcot-Marie-Tooth disease, axonal, type 2S (CMT2S), a rare, serious, and progressive inherited neurological disorder. The designation was granted by the FDA's Office of Orphan Products Development and Of

title: 我國原創RNA編輯技術LEAPER首次應用於杜氏肌營養不良症臨床治療取得突破_脈搏聚合站
summary:
北京大學魏文勝教授團隊聯合昌平實驗室於2026年6月10日在《細胞》發表重要研究成果，展示了我國原創RNA編輯技術LEAPER在治療杜氏肌營養不良症（DMD）這一嚴重遺傳性罕見病中的臨床應用突破。這不僅是我國RNA編輯技術首次進入臨床研究階段並獲積極療效信號，也是國際首次將RNA編輯策略成功應用於DMD患者。
...
杜氏肌營養不良症是一種由DMD基因突變引起的嚴重遺傳性肌肉疾病，患者多在兒童時期發病，表現為逐漸加劇的肌無力和運動功能下降，最終因呼吸或心力衰竭危及生命。我國DMD患者數量居世界前列，累計確診患者約7至9萬人。目前臨床治療僅能部分延緩病程，尚無法根本逆轉疾病。
...
與直接修改DNA的基因編輯不同，LEAPER技術通過設計工程化RNA分子，激活細胞內天然RNA編輯酶，調控病變外顯子的剪接，跳過異常區域，使細胞重新合成功能性肌營養不良蛋白。此方法無需遞送外源編輯蛋白，簡化治療體係，減小體內遞送難度，降低免疫反應風險。
...
在非人
...
模型中，研究人員通過AAV
...
狀ADAR招募RNA（circ-arRNA），實現一次給藥後肌營養
...
持續表達超過一年
.

...
维昇药业-B（02561）盘中涨超7%，截至发稿，股价上涨4.7%，现报20.96港元。
...
据维生药业官微消息，近日，在北京罕见病药品保障先行区政策支持下，北京大学第三医院正式引进全球首款且唯一获批的甲状旁腺激素（PTH）替代疗法——帕罗培特立帕肽（palopegteriparatide），用于治疗成人慢性甲状旁腺功能减退症（以下简称“甲旁减”）。此次落地标志着我国罕见病药品保障先行区覆盖版图再扩容，全球创新药在华可及性加速提升，甲旁减治疗正从传统“对症控症”迈向“生理替代”新阶段。
...
此次引进的帕罗培特立帕肽是基于TransCon（暂时连接）技术平台研发的甲状旁腺激素前药，半衰期可达60小时，也是目前全球首款且唯一获批用于成人慢性甲旁减的激素替代疗法。该

title: Through flux and fluidity, FDA keeps coming back to rare disease - BioSpace
summary: ## Even as FDA approvals for biologic therapies fell in the first half of 2026, regulatory experts are optimistic about a turnaround in the rare disease space after the departure of key leaders at the agency. The FDA approved six orphan drugs in the first half of 2026—leaving the agency just six months to match last year’s total of 30. The Center for Biologics Evaluation and Research (CBER)—which regulates gene therapies and therefore many rare disease drugs—approved two orphan drugs in H1, Rock

title: Vertex Eyes Expansion Beyond Cystic Fibrosis with Planned $10B Crinetics Buyout - Genetic Engineering and Biotechnology News
summary: Vertex Pharmaceuticals has agreed to acquire Crinetics Pharmaceuticals for $10 billion cash, the companies said, in a deal that would expand the buyer’s rare disease portfolio beyond its anchor indication of cystic fibrosis (CF), by adding an approved treatment and a pipeline anchored by two Phase III candidates, all predicted to generate more than $5 billion in annual revenue. The company’s first marketed drug Palsonify® (paltusotine), an oral SST2 agonist, was approved by the FDA in September

title: Study reveals why patients with rare leukemia develop resistance to tagraxofusp - News-Medical
summary: # Study reveals why patients with rare leukemia develop resistance to tagraxofusp. Researchers at The University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center have identified why some patients with a rare type of leukemia, called blastic plasmacytoid dendritic cell neoplasm (BPDCN), eventually develop resistance to tagraxofusp, the first Food and Drug Administration-approved treatment for this disease. The findings are the result of a molecular analysis of previously published results from a Phase 2 trial

title: Otsuka's Voyxact faces rival as FDA okays Vera IgAN drug - pharmaphorum
summary: **Vera Therapeutics has won FDA approval for Trutakna, a BAFF/APRIL inhibitor for IgA nephropathy (IgAN) that will compete with Otsuka's recently approved anti-APRIL drug Voyxact.**. Trutakna (atacicept) has been granted an accelerated approval to reduce proteinuria in adults with primary IgAN who are at risk of disease progression – the same indication as Voyxact (sibeprenlimab), which became the first anti-APRIL drug to reach the US market last year. Trutakna is the first dual BAFF/APRIL drug

title: ASCO 2026: New hope in AL amyloidosis, with Dr Hans Lee - pharmaphorum
summary: # ASCO 2026: New hope in AL amyloidosis, with Dr Hans Lee. A phase 1/2 trial in the rare disease light chain amyloidosis saw 100% of patients obtaining at least a very good partial response with 90% obtaining a complete response. It’s exciting news in an orphan indication which currently lacks any approved treatment, and Dr Hans Lee, director of multiple myeloma research at the Sarah Cannon Research Institute, joined pharmaphorum at ASCO 2026 to talk about the LINKER-AL2 trial, the bispecific an

title: UK first in Europe to clear Boehringer's IPF drug Jascayd - pharmaphorum
summary: # UK first in Europe to clear Boehringer's IPF drug Jascayd. **Boehringer Ingelheim has the first European approval for its new idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis (IPF) therapy, Jascayd, after the UK's MHRA gave a green light to the drug.**. It is the first new treatment approved in the UK for IPF – a disease that has a five-year survival rate of around 46%, comparable to some cancers – in more than a decade. First-in-class Jascayd is a successor to Boehringer's blockbuster kinase inhibitor Ofev (nin
